text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr A. Rahman  
Riverside Health Centre  
114 Millbank, London SW1P 4QP  

Dear Dr A. Rahman,                                                                                                         

Daniel Hughes, DOB: 22-11-1991, Hospital No: H738225 NHS No. 9483726150  
Flat 3, 9 Wren Street, London, WC1X 0HF  

Epilepsy 

Medication: Initiated Levetiracetam August 2025

Levetiracetam 500 mg twice daily (patient confirms adherence; no missed doses in the past month)

Levetiracetam Level: 

28 September 2025: 14 µg/mL (reference 6–46 µg/mL). Dose 500 mg twice daily

Seizures: Patient reports three brief events over six weeks, each with lateral tongue soreness on waking and damp nightwear consistent with possible nocturnal episodes. No clear auras or recalls; partner notes brief nocturnal stiffening and post-event fatigue.

I conducted a video consultation today. Sleep deprivation appears a trigger. We discussed safety, SUDEP awareness, and seizure first aid. I have requested an EEG and ambulatory sleep-deprived study, and arranged an MRI brain with epilepsy protocol. We will maintain current dose pending results and review in eight weeks. The patient has been advised on DVLA guidance and to avoid swimming alone and heights. 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: Patient reports three brief events over six weeks

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: "three brief events over six weeks." This provides a count and a time window. Compute frequency: 3 events / 6 weeks = 0.5 per week. No other seizure frequencies are given, and the patient is not seizure-free. Therefore, the current seizure frequency is 0.5 per week.
seizure_frequency_number: Patient reports three brief events over six weeks

flaws.description: Gold (0.5 per week) disagrees with Reference (unknown)
